topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Policy and Programs
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing specific policy areas including manufacturing, education, energy, government efficiency, and privacy regulations.

Document 1: Directs the President to develop a comprehensive national manufacturing strategy.
an evaluation of the global competitiveness of U.S. manufacturing.
Document 2: Authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants.
to provide interdisciplinary training in integrated health care settings.
Document 3: Requires U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to complete the digitization of all remaining paper-based fingerprint records.
for inclusion in the Automated Biometric Identification System.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code for the renewable electricity production tax credit.
to allow an Indian tribe to assign production from a renewable energy facility.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2107 the energy tax credit.
for investment in qualified small wind energy property.
Document 6: Establishes the Federal Realignment and Closure Commission.
to assess the effectiveness and accountability of agency programs.
Document 7: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual written privacy policy notice requirement.
any financial institution which provides nonpublic personal information only in accordance with specified requirements.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Medicare and Medicaid programs aimed at expanding coverage and improving healthcare services.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to include podiatrists as physicians in order to cover their services under the Medicaid program.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend the period reasonable cost reimbursement contracts under Medicare can remain in the market.
Document 3: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to repeal the exclusion from medical assistance under the Medicaid program of items and services for patients in an institution for mental diseases.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to prohibit Medicare Prescription Drug Plan sponsors from retroactively reducing payment on clean claims submitted by pharmacies.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to rename the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission (MedPAC) and revise its membership requirements.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend through 2013 the minimum payment amount for dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry (DXA) services for bone mass measurement.
Document 7: Amends the Medicare Prescription Drug, Improvement, and Modernization Act of 2003 to extend federal reimbursement of emergency health services furnished to undocumented aliens.

topic.name: Education Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving various aspects of education, including funding, teacher support, and health and safety in schools.

Document 1: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require secure login for online education programs.
Document 2: Allocates funds to support elementary, secondary, and postsecondary education.
Document 3: Expands loan forgiveness for public school teachers under the TEACH Act.
Document 4: Permits federal funds for opportunity scholarships in the D.C. Voucher program.
Document 5: Allows grants to reduce student-to-school nurse ratios.
Document 6: Directs EPA to update radon testing guidelines in schools.
Document 7: Establishes a program for zero-interest loans for biomass energy conversion in educational institutions.

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Modifications
topic.description: This cluster addresses amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning the temporary suspension or modification of duties on various products.

Document 1: modifies the article description relating to the duty-free treatment of U.S. products returned
returned after being exported to include products previously exported
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on thermoplastic biodegradable polymer blend
Document 3: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 5-chloro-3-hydroxy-2-methoxy-2-naphthanilide
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain surge protective receptacles
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-amino-6-niotrophenol-4-sulfonic acid
Document 6: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 4 Chloro Aniline
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on certain footwear

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Healthcare
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to benefits and healthcare services for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to enter into an agreement with the Secretary of Defense for the transfer of data to provide members of the Armed Forces and veterans with individualized information concerning veterans' benefits.
Requires the Secretary to notify the member or veteran of such benefits.
Document 2: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a veterans health care stamp to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans.
Document 3: Reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses by veterans suffering the loss of hands, arms, or legs as a result of military service.
Authorizes additional veterans' disability compensation for service-disabled veterans.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to accept as sufficient proof of service-connection of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) a diagnosis by a mental health professional.
Requires the Secretary to resolve every reasonable doubt in favor of the veteran.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide medical care to a family member of a disabled veteran who provides caregiver services to the veteran.
Prohibits the caregiver from being subject to deductibles, premiums, copayments, or other fees for such care.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure that veterans who are eligible for hospital care and medical services have access to at least one full-service VA medical center in the state.
Directs the Secretary to report to Congress on compliance with such requirement.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to seek to enter into a contract with a non-Department of Veterans Affairs facility to provide hospital care and medical services to veterans.

topic.name: Offshore Energy Policy
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the exploration, production, and environmental management of oil and gas resources in the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Maximize Offshore Resource Exploration Act of 2008
prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from granting an oil or natural gas lease for any OCS located within 25 miles of a state coastline
Document 2: Blowout Prevention Act of 2010
prohibits the Secretary of Energy from issuing a permit to drill for a covered well unless the applicant demonstrates safety measures
Document 3: Oil Pollution Taxpayer and Environment Protection Act
increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%
Document 4: OPEC Accountability Act
requires the President to initiate consultations with oil-producing countries
Document 5: allows any area of the Outer Continental Shelf for which expenditure of federal funds for oil and gas leasing is prohibited to be leased
Document 6: Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Improvement Act of 2011
amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act of 2004
Document 7: Outer Continental Shelf Production Incentive Fee Act
direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases

topic.name: Immigration and Military Service
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the immigration status and naturalization of military personnel and their families, as well as provisions for various immigrant categories.

Document 1: any person who serves or has served under honorable conditions as a member of the Armed Forces shall be eligible for naturalization

an alien who is eligible for a family-sponsored immigrant visa and is either the spouse or child of a permanent resident alien who is serving in the Armed Forces shall be exempt from worldwide immigrant visa numerical limitations.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act with respect to V-visa nonimmigrants to include certain Haitian nationals
Authorizes the Secretary of State to use secondary evidence to verify eligibility for such status.
Document 3: establish a nonimmigrant W-1 visa for an alien whose status in the United States is on a conditional basis
has earned a U.S. high school diploma or obtained a U.S. general education development certificate and is enlisted, or is intending to enlist in the Armed Forces.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at monitoring and assessing foreign countries' development and deployment of electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
Identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
Identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses the designation and management of wilderness areas and conservation lands in the United States, particularly focusing on Colorado and Nevada.

Document 1: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado administered by the BLM, Forest Service, and Bureau of Reclamation as wilderness and as components of the NWPS.
Document 3: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Releases BLM land in any part of the Blue Lakes and Alder Creek wilderness study areas not designated as wilderness by this Act from further study for wilderness designation.
Document 6: Establishes the Gold Butte National Conservation Area in Nevada.

Designates specified wilderness areas administered by the National Park Service (NPS) or the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Clark County, Nevada, as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 7: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Amends the Indian Peaks Wilderness Area, the Arapaho National Recreation Area and the Oregon Islands Wilderness Area Act to adjust the boundaries of the Indian Peaks Wilderness Area.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the welfare and protection of children, particularly in cases of abuse, neglect, and domestic violence.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Civil Rights Act to give Indian tribes criminal jurisdiction over domestic violence
Securing the rights of victims of such crimes.
Document 2: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to direct HHS to make grants for educational campaigns regarding mandatory reporting of child abuse
State responsibility to accept and investigate reports of known and suspected child abuse or neglect.
Document 3: Revises eligibility requirements for adoption assistance and foster care maintenance payments
Provides children with family preservation services, family support services, and adoption promotion.
Document 4: Prohibits a state from having laws that allow use of deadly force without a duty to retreat, except in domestic violence cases
Provides for compliance procedures for states.
Document 5: Requires states to provide notice of placement decisions to relatives of children removed from custody
Allows relatives to request documentation of the reasons for placement decisions.
Document 6: Reauthorizes the adoption incentive payment program
Establishes national standards for home studies for evaluating potential foster and adoptive parents.
Document 7: Directs the Assistant Secretary for Children and Families to require minimum standards for residential programs serving children
Establishes a process for investigating and evaluating reports of child abuse and neglect.

topic.name: Mental Health and Support Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving mental health services, support systems, and protective measures for vulnerable populations, including individuals with autism, older adults, and those with mental illnesses.

Document 1: establish a demonstration program to award grants to eligible entities to develop an autism navigator program
to train first responders in assisting individuals with autism
Document 2: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to initiate and sustain mental health first aid training programs
training on action plans and protocols for referral to such resources
Document 3: demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings
support the establishment and maintenance of interdisciplinary geriatric mental health outreach teams
Document 4: ensure that all programs funded under such Act include appropriate training in elder abuse prevention
increases public awareness of elder abuse and financial exploitation
Document 5: provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses who have co-occurring primary care conditions
to award matching grants to states, territories, and Indian tribes
Document 6: provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions
support services for individuals released from inpatient or residential institutions

topic.name: FDA Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving the regulatory framework for drugs and medical devices under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow the holder of an approved abbreviated new drug application to change the labeling of a drug.
Allows conforming changes to be ordered to the labeling of the equivalent listed drug.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to declare that nothing in it shall prevent or restrict the importation, distribution, or sale of investigational drugs for terminally ill patients.
Prohibits the FDA Commissioner from requiring the disclosure, collection, or reporting of certain information concerning such drugs.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to designate a combination of drugs as a significant drug combination.
Requires the Secretary to establish an interagency task force to encourage the co-development of drugs in significant drug combinations.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of the Treasury to destroy imported counterfeit drugs.
Establishes civil monetary penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act by requiring a manufacturer of a medical device to give the FDA premarket notification of changes to the design or reprocessing instructions of its device.
Bans the devices if the manufacturers violate the notification requirements.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to reauthorize and establish new FDA prescription drug user-fee programs.
Requires the Secretary to submit a report on the progress of the FDA in achieving the goals identified.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend by six months the exclusivity period for a drug or biological product approved by the FDA.
Limits a product to one extension under this Act.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative acts related to the planning, construction, and management of water resources and ecosystem restoration projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act
authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with Pima County, Arizona, to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities
enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration
water quality control, water supply storage, outdoor recreation enhancements

topic.name: Housing Finance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving housing finance, mortgage insurance, and foreclosure mitigation in the United States.

Document 1: Making Home Affordable Improvements Act - Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to provide assistance in specified amounts to the National Foreclosure Mitigation Counseling Program for foreclosure mitigation counseling activities.

Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to revise HAMP guidelines to provide for monthly collection by the Secretary of the Treasury from each participating mortgage servicer and lender of comprehensive data on its activities.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act and the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to make permanent the increase in the standard maximum deposit insurance amount.
Amends the National Housing Act to revise certain requirements for the HOPE for Homeowners Program.
Document 3: FHA Emergency Fiscal Solvency Act of 2013 - Amends the National Housing Act to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for the first 11 years of the term of an insured mortgage.
Directs the Secretary to establish a process for mortgagees to appeal indemnification determinations.
Document 4: FHA Taxpayer Protection Act of 2009 - Amends the National Housing Act to increase the downpayment requirement for a Federal Housing Administration mortgage loan from 3.5% to 5%.
Directs the Comptroller General to study and report to Congress on the Mutual Mortgage Insurance Fund.
Document 5: Residential Mortgage Market Privatization and Standardization Act of 2011 - Amends the National Housing Act and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Act to require the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to begin reducing by at least 10% per year the percentage of the value of a trust certificate or other security that may be guaranteed by Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac.
Directs each agency to require that all entities they regulate put into the public domain reasonably accurate and complete data relating to covered mortgage loans.
Document 6: Reverse Mortgage Stabilization Act of 2013 - Amends the National Housing Act, with respect to mortgage insurance for home equity conversion mortgages of elderly homeowners, to authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish any additional or alternative requirements determined necessary to improve the fiscal safety and soundness of the reverse mortgage program.
Document 7: Preserving Access to Manufactured Housing Act - Amends the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 to exclude a seller of manufactured homes from the definition of loan originator.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to revise the definition of 'high cost mortgage.'

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation, protection, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out, either individually or cooperatively, prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Establishes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions that are designed to preserve and protect coral reef ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy, revised as necessary.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to other appropriate projects.
Authorizes the Administrator of NOAA to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.

topic.name: Rural Education and Agricultural Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving educational opportunities and agricultural support in rural areas.

Document 1: Amends part B (Rural Education Initiative) of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
gives rural local educational agencies (LEAs) federal formula grants and greater flexibility in the use of state educational funds.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to make a supplemental direct commodity payment to eligible producers
provide supplemental assistance to eligible producers and first-handlers of the 2009 crop of cottonseed in disaster counties.
Document 3: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act
direct the Secretary of Agriculture to base the minimum Class II milk price on the average production cost of producing all milk.
Document 4: Extends and revises various agricultural programs
including programs respecting: milk and dairy, conservation security, food stamps.
Document 5: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to designate as bioenergy cropsheds areas that are physically located within a 50-mile radius
to provide bioenergy crop transition assistance to encourage the producers to produce bioenergy crops.
Document 6: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend through FY2014 the benefit calculation of 113.6 % of the June 2008 value of the thrifty food plan
for the supplemental nutrition assistance program (SNAP, formerly known as food stamps).
Document 7: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to make foster children categorically eligible for free lunches
provided the appropriate state or local agency provides the children's local educational agency with documentation regarding their status as foster children.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Efficiency Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy technologies, improving energy efficiency, and reducing petroleum dependency.

Document 1: Amends the National Oilheat Research Alliance Act of 2000 to require the National Oilheat Research Alliance to provide the Secretary of Energy (DOE) with a list of qualified nominees for Alliance membership
research to develop renewable fuels; and examine the compatibility of different renewable fuels with oilheat fuel utilization equipment
Document 2: encourage and accelerate the use of non-petroleum based fuels, alternative fuel vehicles, and other advanced vehicle technologies
provide training, technical assistance, and tools to end-users that adopt petroleum reduction technologies
Document 3: carry out a research, development, and technology demonstration program to improve the efficiency of gas turbines
support first-of-a-kind engineering and detailed gas turbine design for megawatt-scale and utility-scale electric power generation
Document 4: revise renewable fuel standards for calendar years 2006-2020
require the Secretary to establish an incentives program for production of cellulosic ethanol and other emerging biofuels derived from renewable sources
Document 5: conduct a program of research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of technologies needed for the development of plug-in hybrid electric vehicles
establish a competitive grant pilot demonstration program
Document 6: establish a Clean Energy Technology Manufacturing and Export Assistance Fund
promote policies that will reduce production costs and encourage innovation, investment, and productivity in the clean energy technology sector
Document 7: establish a program to provide interest-free loans to eligible institutions for capital costs for converting existing equipment
ensure that an institution receiving such a loan will use renewable biomass for no less than 75% of the energy generation, heating, or cooling needs

topic.name: Consumer Data Privacy Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding personally identifiable information and establishing protocols for data security and breach notifications.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the federal criminal code authorizing a video tape service provider to disclose personally identifiable information concerning any consumer
require such consent be in a form distinct and separate from any form setting forth other legal or financial obligations of the consumer
Document 2: Requires any entity that collects, sells, discloses for consideration, or uses personally identifiable information to provide consumers notice
establish a privacy policy with respect to the collection, sale, disclosure for consideration, dissemination, use, and security of consumers' personally identifiable information
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information
Document 4: Establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security involving disclosure of sensitive personal information
Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach
Document 6: Requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data
Requires a covered entity to give notice of any breach of security that the entity reasonably believes has caused or will cause identity theft
Document 5: directs the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies to provide to individuals whose personally identifiable information may have been compromised

topic.name: Tax Regulation and Compliance
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating tax practices, enhancing accountability, and protecting taxpayers from fraud and abuse.

Document 1: Prohibits the payment of any performance award to an employee of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) who owes an outstanding federal tax debt.
No Bonuses for Tax Delinquent IRS Employees Act of 2014
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to regulate tax return preparers who are not otherwise subject to regulation.
Tax Return Preparer Accountability Act of 2015
Document 3: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to disseminate information on fraud targeting seniors.
Senior Financial Empowerment Act of 2012
Document 4: Directs the IRS to provide a taxpayer selected for an audit sufficient information regarding the reason for the audit.
Biased IRS Audit Systems Prevention Act
Document 5: Revises requirements for anti-money laundering programs with respect to federally insured depository institutions.
Money Service Business Act of 2009
Document 6: Directs the Commissioner of Social Security to issue uniform standards for the protection of Social Security numbers.
Safeguarding Social Security Numbers Act of 2009
Document 7: Prohibits IRS from initiating any new audit of any taxpayer for 180 days after enactment.
HALT the IRS Act

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at regulating and overseeing banking institutions and financial systems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Commodity Exchange Act to make the Federal Housing Finance Agency the appropriate federal banking agency for any Federal Home Loan Bank
exempting any Federal Home Loan Bank loan from regulation as a swap
Document 2: Requires federal banking agencies to establish capital requirements for the ratio of equity capital to total consolidated assets for all financial institutions

Prohibits such requirements from requiring a financial institution with more than $50 billion in total consolidated assets to have a ratio of less than 8% of equity capital to total consolidated assets
Document 3: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to direct the Chairman of the Federal Open Market Committee to submit to the Comptroller General
Requires the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to testify before certain congressional committees
Document 4: directs the Comptroller of the Currency, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, and the FDIC to promulgate a regulation exempting community banks from regulations implementing 'Basel III'
exempts from the internal control attestation requirements of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
Document 5: directs the Financial Stability Oversight Council to compile and submit to the Secretary of the Treasury a list of entities that the Council deems Too Big To Fail
break up entities on the List so that their failure would no longer cause a catastrophic effect upon the U.S. or global economy
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prescribe requirements for deposit restricted qualified tuition programs
Defines such a program as one in which the cash provided to it by a contributor may be invested only in deposits insured by the FDIC
Document 7: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act and the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to exempt from certain regulatory requirements any swaps and security-based swaps
Requires that such exempted agreements, contracts, or transactions be reported to an appropriate data repository

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife and natural resources, including hunting regulations and protection of species.

Document 1: Prevents soring of horses to ensure humane treatment in shows and exhibitions.
Establishes inspection and enforcement procedures for horse welfare.
Document 2: Amends budget acts to support wildlife restoration and sport fishing.
Grants authority for electronic duck stamps to enhance hunting access.
Document 3: Reauthorizes programs for wildlife habitat conservation and public access for recreation.
Prioritizes land acquisitions to improve access to hunting and fishing.
Document 4: Allows importation of legally harvested polar bear parts to support conservation efforts.
Document 5: Amends the Endangered Species Act to manage gray wolf populations at the state level.
Document 6: Reauthorizes the Junior Duck Stamp program to promote conservation education.
Document 7: Authorizes price adjustments for Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps to ensure funding stability.

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures and amendments related to grants, education, health insurance, postal designations, environmental regulations, and financial regulations.

Document 1: Bulletproof Vest Partnership Grant Program Reauthorization Act of 2013
authorize appropriations for the Bulletproof Vest Partnership Grant Program
Document 2: Family Engagement in Education Act of 2015
authorize states to reserve school improvement funds
Document 3: Keep Your Health Plan Act of 2013
permits a health insurance issuer to continue offering such coverage
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
as the 'James 'Jim' Kohnen Post Office'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
as the 'Thaddeus Stevens Post Office'
Document 6: prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency from requiring a National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System permit
for a vessel that is less than 79 feet in length
Document 7: Relief for America's Main Street Lenders Act
amends the Commodity Exchange Act and the Securities Exchange Act

topic.name: Rail and Infrastructure Investment
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the funding, improvement, and regulation of rail and transportation infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Makes supplemental appropriations for FY2010 to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for state and tribal assistance grants
capital investments in surface transportation infrastructure.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2013 for Amtrak capital and operating grants
grants to Amtrak and states participating in the Next Generation Corridor Train Equipment Pool Committee.
Document 3: allow a tax credit through 2012 for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property
extend through 2012 the tax credit for qualified railroad track maintenance expenditures.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a program to eliminate duplicative environmental reviews
Authorize a state to use state environmental review and approval laws and procedures.
Document 5: This bill authorizes Bakken crude oil to be transported by rail
maximum volatility set by the New York Mercantile Exchange for crude oil futures contracts.
Document 6: Establishes the National Commission on Energy Security and Transition to New Fuels
grants to federal and private sector researchers to research and develop alternative fuels.
Document 7: directs the Federal Railroad Administration to establish a local rail facilities and safety program
A grant awarded under the program may be used for a capital project to mitigate the impacts of rail infrastructure.

topic.name: STEM Education and Innovation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at enhancing education and research in STEM fields, promoting coding and computer programming, and supporting technological innovation.

Document 1: importance of instruction in coding and computer programming to students' academic and vocational success
a national strategy to ensure competitiveness in emerging science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields
Document 2: forgive students' outstanding principal on such interest-free loans if they maintained a certain grade point average or majored in a science, technology, engineering, or mathematics (STEM) field
Document 4: awarding grants to institutions of higher education for the implementation or expansion of research-based reforms in master's and doctoral level STEM education
Document 5: expand the scope of the National Nanotechnology Program
promote the rapid commercial development of nanoscale-enabled devices, systems, and technologies
Document 6: establish a Nanomanufacturing Investment Partnership to provide funding for precommercial nanomanufacturing research and development projects
Document 7: establish standards and best practices for forensic science disciplines
promoting standards and best practices and ensuring scientific validity, reliability, and accuracy of forensic testing

topic.name: Health and Retirement Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving health insurance coverage, retirement savings, and financial support for individuals and families.

Document 1: prohibit a group health plan or health insurance issuer from denying benefits for recreational injuries
treatment of an injury solely because it resulted from participation in a legal mode of transportation
Document 2: allow an additional tax deduction for amounts paid to the HSA of a taxpayer's child or grandchild
increase the maximum HSA contribution limit
Document 3: require certain employers to make available to their eligible employees a payroll deposit individual retirement account
impose a penalty on employers who fail to provide access to an automatic IRA arrangement
Document 4: allow tax-free distributions from a Coverdell education savings account for first-time homebuyer expenses
permit rollovers from Coverdell education savings accounts to Roth individual retirement accounts
Document 5: require a health plan providing benefits with respect to anticancer medications to provide no less favorable coverage
prohibits a health plan from imposing an increase in out-of-pocket costs for anticancer medications
Document 6: exclude from gross income amounts distributed from tax-exempt retirement plans to acquire an ownership interest in an entity
in connection with beginning an active trade or business
Document 7: remove all limitations on COBRA continuation coverage
provides no deadline for discontinuing such coverage

topic.name: Support for Small Businesses
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing financial assistance and support programs for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994 to require the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund to make grants to community development financial institutions
Defines 'small business loan program' as a program in which a community development financial institution offers loans to small businesses
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the women's business centers program
Requires the SBA to establish center performance measures
Document 3: Directs the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a grant program for small business development centers
Requires program funding to be used for the development of business advisory capacity
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to extend through September 27, 2013 the authority for Small Business Administration (SBA) refinancing of small business debt
Document 5: Establish within the Small Business Administration (SBA) an Office of Minority Small Business Development
Authorizes the SBA Administrator to make grants to eligible educational institutions
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make disaster loans to assist eligible small businesses
Limits loan amounts to $250,000
Document 7: Increases funding for the Office of Veterans Business Development of the Small Business Administration (SBA)

The bill sets forth the types of bad faith representations, assertions of legal action, claims of a sender holding an exclusive license, compensation requests, or omissions that are considered to be unfair or deceptive.
Document 2: Prohibits, as an unfair and deceptive act or practice and an unfair method of competition in or affecting interstate commerce, any person from being a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim.
Authorizes the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to exempt agreements in furtherance of market competition and for the benefit of consumers.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to establish a program of grants to states to investigate and prosecute misleading and fraudulent marketing practices.
Develop educational materials and training aimed at reducing misleading and fraudulent marketing of financial products toward seniors.
Document 4: Establishes the Consumer Financial Protection Agency as an independent executive agency to regulate the provision of consumer financial products or services.
Requires the Agency to seek to promote transparency, simplicity, fairness, accountability, and access in the market for consumer financial products or services.
Document 5: Prohibits any person who is a licensed health care service provider but who is not a medical doctor from making any statement or engaging in any act that deceives or misleads the public.
Treats such statements or acts as unfair or deceptive acts or practices prescribed under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
Document 6: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish specified security policies and procedures.
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses.
Document 7: Makes it unlawful to acquire or use customer proprietary network information without written consent.

Prohibits a commercial mobile services provider from providing any customer's wireless telephone number information to any wireless directory assistance service without express prior customer authorization.
